REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO EXPUNGE PLAINTIFF'S MECHANICS LIEN IS GRANTED. MECHANIC'S LIEN IS EXPUNGED. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO CARRY THEIR BURDEN AND CITED NO CASES FOR THE PROPOSITION THAT CC 8412 REQUIREMENTS HAVE BEEN MET. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR ATTORNEY'S FEESIS DENIED. DEFENDANT FAILS TO CITE ANY CASES FOR THE PROPOSITION THAT CC 8488 APPLIES UNDER THE FACTS OF THIS CASE. MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-02-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS ANAND GOHEL, CRISSIUS PROPERTIES, LLC AND 3866 CLAY STREET LLC'S MOTION TO STRIKE AMENDED COMPLAINT

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DENIED in part and moot in part. Motion is denied re: failure to file a timely amendment and cause of action for foreclosure on mechanic's lien. Motion is otherwise moot in light of voluntary dismissal. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/CFH)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-02-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS ANAND GOHEL, CRISSIUS PROPERTIES LLC AND 3866 CLAY STREET LLC'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: SUSTAINED in part; OVERRULED in part and moot in part.

Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to alter ego for Plaintiff to allege facts upon which they base "information and belief" in paragraphs 15 and 16.

Demurer is OVERRULED as to the cause of action for foreclosure on mechanic's lien at this time. Defendant cites no authority for the proposition that this Court order, that is subject to a pending appeal, is final. The Court notes that there is no Civil Code section 8494(b). To the extent Defendant meant to refer to CC 8490(b), this code section does not address an appeal.

Demurrer is overruled as to CCP 581(f)(2). This action is stayed pending the resolution of appeal.
Demurrer is otherwise moot in light of the voluntary dismissal.

Plaintiff shall have 10 days from the notice of remittitur to file an amended complaint. Depending on the outcome of appeal, Plaintiff may include the cause of action for foreclosure on mechanic's lien. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/CFH)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Expunge Plaintiff'S Mechanics Lien
Off Calendar Jan-13-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Ronald Beretska; 925-954-9700; Borbely & Associates; counsel for plaintiff appearing via Zoom.
Samuel Ray; 415-579-1413; Colla & Ray LLP; counsel for defendant appearing via Zoom.

Mr. Beretska contests the courts tentative ruling. Argued. The court also ruled on defendant's objections of the evidence in support of motion to expunge mechanics lien. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ANAND GOHEL ALSO KNOWN AS RAMAN GOHEL, AN INDIVIDUAL, CRISSIUS PROPERTIES, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED, 3866 CLAY LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY Notice Of Motion And Motion To Expunge Plaintiff'S Mechanics Lien is GRANTED. Mechanic's lien is expunged. Plaintiff failed to carry their burden and cited no cases for the proposition that CC 8412 requirements have been met. Defendant's request for attorney's fees is denied. Defendant fails to cite any cases for the proposition that CC 8488 applies under the facts of this case.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the court's tentative ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b)
Judge: Charles F, Haines ; Clerk: L. Young ; Not Reported. (D501)
